children.text: The field of West African cinemas has long suffered from a lack of research and scholarship, which deprives those interested in African studies and cultures of valuable information and resources. In dissecting Paulin S Vieyra’s film Afrique sur Seine, I examine the ways in which this film has served African cinemas and discourse. Through the use of AV-Annotate to analyze this piece, I explore how Vieyra reclaims African documentary toward African viewership, in spite of the preexisting colonial ethnographic film. In Afrique sur Seine, Vieyra plants African people firmly in the present moment in Paris, in contrast to the propaganda of colonial film which would portray them as backwards or immature. In staking this claim of personhood, Vieyra also takes hold of the future of African people in a changing world. My analysis through AV-Annotate, an insightful tool for future film enthusiasts and scholars, utilizes cinematographic techniques, action, and film analysis.
